description: Establish the foundational pipeline that connects external design publishes to our internal code generation engine, eliminating manual UI boilerplate coding.
businessValue: Reduces the time from design-approval to first-line-of-code by 90%, freeing up frontend engineers to focus on complex state management rather than pixel-pushing.

storyStatement.feature: the system to securely receive payload events from Figma webhooks
storyStatement.benefit: we can trigger the AI generation pipeline exactly when a designer publishes a library update.

scenarios.scenarioName: Webhook payload contains a valid HMAC signature
given: A POST request is made to /api/v1/webhooks/design-publish
when: The X-Signature header matches the SHA-256 hash of the payload using the Vault secret
then: The system responds with HTTP 202 Accepted
The payload is placed onto the processing queue

scenarios.scenarioName: Webhook payload contains an invalid or missing signature
when: A POST request is received with a mismatched X-Signature header
then: The system responds with HTTP 401 Unauthorized
A security alert is logged to Splunk

metadata.title: Automated PR Creation for Generated Components

storyStatement.feature: the AI engine to automatically open a Pull Request with the generated React code
storyStatement.benefit: I can review, tweak, and merge the component directly in my existing GitHub workflow rather than copy-pasting from a dashboard.

scenarios.scenarioName: AI engine successfully generates a valid AST and Code string
given: The Polyglot Generation Engine has finished processing a node graph
The generated code passes the ESLint AST syntax check
then: The system creates a new branch named 'ai-gen/component-{id}'
The system commits the file to 'src/components/generated/'
A Pull Request is opened against the 'main' branch
The PR description includes a link to the original Figma design node
The GitHub CODEOWNERS team is automatically tagged as reviewers
